content.problemStatement:
Chef just realized he has a ton of homework due tomorrow!

Chef has several worksheets of questions with him. Each worksheet has exactly YYY questions in it.

Chef only has the time to complete **at most 101010** worksheets before the submission deadline.

Chef has previously answered XXX questions, and he needs to answer at least 100100100 questions in total to get a full score for the homework.

Will Chef be able to get a full score?


Input Format

The only line of input will contain two space-separated integers XXX and YYY — the number of questions already answered by Chef, and the number of questions in each worksheet.


Output Format
For each test case, output the answer: `"YES"` (without quotes) if Chef can receive a full score for the homework, and `"NO"` (without quotes) otherwise.


Each character of the output may be printed in either uppercase or lowercase, i.e. the strings `No`, `NO`, `nO`, and `no` will be treated as equivalent.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Chef has already completed X=73X = 73X=73 questions.
Each worksheet has 444 questions. Chef can complete 777 of them for 4⋅7=284\cdot 7 = 284⋅7=28 questions, which when added to the initial 737373 puts him at 101101101 which is more than 100100100.

Explanation:
Chef has already completed X=0X = 0X=0 questions.
Each worksheet has 101010 questions. Chef can complete 101010 of them for 10⋅10=10010\cdot 10 = 10010⋅10=100 questions, which when added to the initial 000 puts him at 100100100 which is enough.

Explanation:
Chef has already completed X=47X = 47X=47 questions.
Each worksheet has 555 questions. Even if Chef completes 101010 of them for 10⋅5=5010\cdot 5 = 5010⋅5=50 questions, he will have a total of 47+50=9747 + 50 = 9747+50=97 done which is not enough.
